mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and declining corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when only one series rises, or forward returns are negative in >55% of co-occurring positive growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers immediate positive repricing in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when supply chain bottlenecks dominate.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation acceleration above 0.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the predictive IC drops below 0.02 when supply-driven inflation dominates.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences, or the relationship breaks down during periods of labor force participation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, driving immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals severe supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, which depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to predict returns when the decline is driven by temporary weather disruptions.
